title: Task Monitor Subscription and Metrics
interaction_flow: ProxyServer → TaskMonitor: new TaskMonitor({ store, logger })
MailboxStore → TaskMonitor: raw JSON string | null
TaskMonitor → TaskMonitor: _restoreStats() — hydrates _stats from persisted state
TaskMonitor → MailboxStore: setState('task_subscription', JSON{enabled:true, filters, subscribed_at})
TaskMonitor → MailboxStore: send({ type:'task_subscribe', payload:{ capability_filter:filters } })
RouteHandler → MailboxStore: send({ type:'task_claim', payload:body, priority:'high' })
Worker → RouteHandler: (implicitly via task fail path)
TaskMonitor → RouteHandler: { subscribed, tasks_received, tasks_claimed, tasks_completed, tasks_failed, tasks_pending, last_claim_at, last_complete_at, avg_completion_ms }
TaskMonitor → LifecycleManager: { task_subscription:bool, task_metrics:{ pending, claimed, completed, failed, avg_completion_ms } }
key_relationships: TaskMonitor depends_on MailboxStore (state persistence, message sending, pending count)
RouteHandler depends_on TaskMonitor (passed as argument to buildRoutes)
LifecycleManager depends_on TaskMonitor (via getTaskMeta callback in ProxyServer)
TaskMonitor guards subscribed getter via store.getState('task_subscription')
method_signatures: recordTaskReceived(count: number = 1): void — in-memory only, no persistence
state_keys: 'task_monitor_stats' — JSON: tasks_claimed, tasks_completed, tasks_failed, tasks_received, last_claim_at, last_complete_at, avg_completion_ms
important_notes: avg_completion_ms uses rolling window of last 100 completion durations
tasks_received is in-memory only (not persisted to store)
All record* methods persist stats to store via setState after updating in-memory _stats
subscribe/unsubscribe both persist to store AND emit a message to the hub via store.send()
RouteHandler falls back to direct store.send() if taskMonitor is null (optional dependency)
getHeartbeatMeta() is wired into LifecycleManager heartbeat via a callback lambda in ProxyServer
